title: Gold Leaves
content:
Lo! I am come to autumn,
    When all the leaves are gold;
    Grey hairs and golden leaves cry out
    The year and I are old.

    In youth I sought the prince of men,
    Captain in cosmic wars,
    Our Titan, even the weeds would show
    Defiant, to the stars.

    But now a great thing in the street
    Seems any human nod,
    Where shift in strange democracy
    The million masks of God.

    In youth I sought the golden flower
    Hidden in wood or wold,
    But I am come to autumn,
    When all the leaves are gold.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Gilbert Keith Chesterton represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Gilbert Keith Chesterton's bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
